speaker: JESSICA
text: I am sorry thou wilt leave my father so.

speaker: JESSICA
text: Our house is hell and thou, a merry devil,

speaker: JESSICA
text: Didst rob it of some taste of tediousness.

speaker: JESSICA
text: But fare thee well. There is a ducat for thee,

speaker: JESSICA
text: And, Lancelet, soon at supper shalt thou see

speaker: JESSICA
text: Lorenzo, who is thy new master’s guest.

speaker: JESSICA
text: Give him this letter, do it secretly,

speaker: JESSICA
text: And so farewell. I would not have my father

speaker: JESSICA
text: See me in talk with thee.

speaker: LANCELET
text: Adieu. Tears exhibit my tongue, most beautiful

speaker: LANCELET
text: pagan, most sweet Jew. If a Christian do not

speaker: LANCELET
text: play the knave and get thee, I am much deceived.

speaker: LANCELET
text: But adieu. These foolish drops do something drown

speaker: LANCELET
text: my manly spirit. Adieu.

speaker: JESSICA
text: Farewell, good Lancelet.

speaker: JESSICA
text: Alack, what heinous sin is it in me

speaker: JESSICA
text: To be ashamed to be my father’s child?

speaker: JESSICA
text: But though I am a daughter to his blood,

speaker: JESSICA
text: I am not to his manners. O Lorenzo,

speaker: JESSICA
text: If thou keep promise, I shall end this strife,

speaker: JESSICA
text: Become a Christian and thy loving wife.
